lines.text: #49 FEB. 23 1953. 3:25 to 3:35 PM. MONTFBEN, FRANCE. 44°, 4°? A
circular metallic, but intensely brilliant object hovered motionless
for nearly 10 minutes, then disappeared vertically. Observed by the
radio navigator in the french air force.
#50 FEB. 23 1953. Noontime. COOS BAY, OREGON. 43 1/2°, 124 1/2°. A cigar-
like object or spherical object with extensions on it, appeared just
to the west of the sun. (COMMENT: This area has many balloon flights,
and it is probable this objects lack of motion would include it among
balloon flights.
#51 FEB. 23 1953. Daytime. Near GOSHEN, OHIO. 39 1/2°, 84 1/2. A metallic
saucer, emitting a white glow at intervals, wobbled as it crossed sky
faster than a jet. Observed by 2 men, one a former A.F. Technician.
#53 FEB. 23 1953. Evening? SOUTHPORT, CONN. 8 Glowing saucers in a bo
formation, hovered 2 minutes, then swiftly disappeared.
#54 FEB. 23 1953. Evening. CONNEAUT, OHIO. 42°, 80 1/2°. Strange light
seen here again, and also at AMHERST, OHIO. No other details availabl
#55 FEB. 23 1953. 4:00 P.M. NIMES, FRANCE. 44°, 4 1/2°. An unknown obje
slowly crossed sky, leaving luminous vapor trail that was at first th
and horizontal, but later curled up in less than a minute, after
becoming shaped like a circle.
#56 FEB.23 1953. Evening. STURGEON BAY, WISCONSIN. 44 3/4°, 87 3/4°. Same
bright light changing colors observed again. (See report #43)
#57 FEB. 24 1953. Morning. ERIE, OHIO. 42 1/8°, 80°. Flying saucer was
seen in area again, by milkman.
#58 FEB. 24 1953. Evening? OLEAN, NEW YORK. 42°, 78 1/2°. A light glowi
in sky changed color from red to orange for short time.
#59 FEB. 25 1953. 11:00 A.M. BLIDA, ALGERIA. 36 1/2°, 2 1/2°. Two unidentif
aircraft or objects, flew noiselessly flew with great speed across sk
leaving a circular white vapor trail path that remained for some time
#60 FEB. 25 1953. Daytime. SERIA, BRUNEI STATE, BORNEO. 5°, 114°. A
circular object hovered in sky 40 minutes, left a vaportrail. (COMMEN
A letter from the Education Dept. director in response to our reques
to the Borneo papers stated this was Venus shining brightly, visible
noon time the next day also. The exceptional duration tends to back t
conventional theories of a star or balloon, although news versions see
to describe a usual Rotating, circular saucer)
#61 FEB. 26 1953. Morning. PERIGEUX, FRANCE. 45°, 1°. A brilliant
cylindrical object, followed by a shapeless object appeared in sky. T
very large shapeless object moved away with great speed, noiselessly,
and the pole-like or cigar shaped object turned towards city and move
across part of sky for 1 minute. (COMMENT: This appears to be a rare
report of a saucer seen with the cigar-shaped objects, first reported
over Sweden in 1946. The eyewitnesses said object moved in different
directions, and moved too fast to have been clouds of any sort.)
#62 FEB. 27 1953. 6:05 AM. MONROE TOWNSHIP, OHIO. 42°, 80 1/2°. A very
brilliant yellow white light, elliptical or eyeshaped, hovered for 2
minutes in the western sky. It appeared about '8 inches' across,
and had the brilliance of a welding flame, but not the color.
